title: Consumer Lifecycle, Cancellation, Readiness, and Graceful Shutdown · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for consumer lifecycle, cancellation, readiness, and graceful shutdown and requires the smallest defensible consumer registration, readiness, and shutdown state machine with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for consumer registration, readiness, and shutdown state machine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
Track consumer tags and channel generations so stale cancellation, delivery, and close events cannot mutate current state.
Handle a null delivery callback and cancellation notifications as lifecycle events with cause, readiness, recovery, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Consume Registration Readiness · changed case · Quiz Consume Registration R…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 1186 in a payment-effect relay after an ambiguous provider timeout. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to sequence readiness, cancellation, in-flight draining, deadline, and channel close; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Report ready only after connection, topology, channel, prefetch, consume registration, and required dependencies are ready.
